comments.author: @globerealife
comments.text: It is fascinating how fast Sweden is moving away from physical money, but I can't help but wonder what happens if the power grid or internet suddenly goes down. Do you think pushing for a completely cashless society is actually a safe move in the long run?

comments.author: @GlobeTravel2026
comments.text: Treating prisoners with that level of respect and giving them college-like dorms sounds completely backward to our system, yet their repeat offense rates are significantly lower.

comments.author: @iansaga93
comments.text:
Tar inte upp att det är skjutningar och sprängningar mm som händer varje dag pga att all invandring. Det är ju 100% av dom som gör det och beställer sånt är första eller tredje generationens invandrare.
Jag märkte det redan på 80 talet hur detta med invandring och det skulle gå.
Dom som har bestämt det har ju inte haft invandarförläggning mm i deras områden och vi svenskar har blivit mindre värda i vårt egna land.
Tom flyktingar från Ukraina 🇺🇦 får mindre dag ersättning än andra ” flyktingar ” , invandrare från andra länder.

replies.author: @TheGlobalTruth04
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your perspective. This is a complex issue with many different opinions. I hope we can keep the discussion respectful and based on facts, while listening to each other's experiences. 🙏

comments.author: @uncensoredworldtravel
comments.text: It’s wild how a society can achieve such high levels of independence and freedom, yet struggle so deeply with social isolation. The trade-off is real.

replies.author: @TheGlobalTruth04
replies.text: A thoughtful observation. Every society has its own strengths and challenges, and balancing personal independence with a strong sense of community isn't always easy. It's an ongoing conversation, and there's certainly a lot we can learn from different cultures.

comments.author: @peterroodt5343
comments.text: Been there , very strange , nobody has an opinion

replies.author: @TheGlobalTruth04
replies.text: Thanks for sharing your experience. Every visitor comes away with different impressions, and no single experience can represent an entire country or its people. We appreciate hearing different perspectives.

comments.author: @UnseenWorldDocs_Us
comments.text: More than half of Stockholm households live completely alone? 📜 Ultimate freedom or a lonely crisis? 🤔👇

replies.author: @UnseenWorldDocs_Us
replies.text: @TheGlobalTruth04 Spot on breakdown! True independence shouldn't mean sacrificing basic human bond! ❤

replies.author: @UnseenWorldDocs_Us

replies.author: @Henriks_Trafikskola
replies.text: Are you a introvert beeing alone is a nice, for a extrovert it can be a pain

replies.author: @TheGlobalTruth04
replies.text: It’s a fascinating contrast. Living alone can represent independence, privacy, and personal freedom—but it can also increase the risk of isolation. The real question may be whether society can protect individual choice while still creating strong opportunities for connection and community.

comments.author: @mfredholm
comments.text:
This video is 80% stolen material from Jonna Jinton and her youtube video "Living with the Dark Winters in Sweden".
And the rest is just some dude guessing about what life in Sweden is like while having no idea about how life really is like here in Sweden.

comments.text: Sweden's balance of progress and personal independence is fascinating, but the loneliness mentioned is a real contrast. It's a beautiful, orderly nation that clearly values its own space. Thoughtful watch.
